Deletionists, please do not delete this section, because the "Extraterrestrial oceans" exists in this article, do not discriminate me as an editor from editors who wrote the "Extraterrestrial oceans" section, because discrimination on Wikipedia is unacceptable. Or, delete all sections about non-Earth oceans and change the name of this article to "Oceans on Earth"!

/* Depth */ removed content that had a low quality source (see also on talk page). Also this seems to me overly detailed, almost like trivia - do we really need it here? Might fit better at a different Wikipedia article.
/* Earth's outer core */ moved to talk page due to the same concern that I have explained in my previous edit.

/* Magma oceans */ moved but I am not convinced this content is needed here. We don't need to mention any kind of term that has the word "ocean" in it, when the core topic of this article is clearly spelled out in the first sentence: salty water on Earth.

Clarification

/* pH */ changed based on discussion with Tim Jickells (also to bring in line with text that comes later in the climate change section). Tim wrote to me: "Here the issue is that scientists (like me) never say anything absolutely definitive!!. In my defence, it is possible in theory that in some areas climate change may locally increase ocean mixing and decrease deoxygenation pressures, but generally climate change is going to increase deoxygenation."

/* top */ Edits to improve readability, done in consultation with my astrophysicist husband. Asked if it was accurate to say that solar radiation as "falling on the ocean" he said no.  "reaches the ocean" is more accurate. I warded off his request to point out that the direct grazing incidence at the equator vs the poles has an impact on temperature.

/* top */  revising sentences that being  with "it" to make what "it" refers to more clear to the average reader.  Also taking out the word "region" to avoid readers thinking in terms of different geographical regions, as opposed to the areas of the water column. Doesn't make sense to have a full paragraph about a topic on Astrooceanography in the lead of "ocean" if that content is the topic of a sub-article.
added a sentence to the lead which summarises one of the sections from the article.

I have created a section on terminology which we can improve over time. I have moved content about terminology from the lead to to this section as I don't think it needs to take up that much room in the lead. The content here was copied from [[world ocean]] and [[sea]]. See those pages for attribution history.
